name: claws_list
description: List all open VS Code terminals with their ID, name, PID, shell integration status, active state, and pty log path (null if not wrapped). Use this to discover what's running before sending commands.

name: claws_create
description: Create a new VS Code terminal. Set wrapped=true to enable full pty logging — this lets you read everything that happens in the terminal including TUI sessions (Claude Code, vim, htop, REPLs). The terminal appears visibly in VS Code's panel.

wrapped.description: Enable script(1) pty logging for full read-back. Defaults to true — all worker terminals should be wrapped for observability. Set false only for interactive UI terminals where logging is undesired.

name: claws_send
description: Send text into a terminal. The text arrives at whatever input is active — shell prompt, TUI input field, REPL prompt. Multi-line text is auto-wrapped in bracketed paste mode. Set newline=false to send raw keystrokes without Enter.

id.description: Terminal ID from claws_list or claws_create

name: claws_exec
description: Execute a shell command in a terminal and capture the output (stdout + stderr + exit code). Uses file-based capture — works in any terminal type. Waits for the command to finish.

name: claws_read_log
description: Read a wrapped terminal's pty log with ANSI escapes stripped. Returns clean, readable text of everything that happened — including TUI sessions, build output, AI coding assistant transcripts. Only works for terminals created with wrapped=true.

lines.description: Number of lines to return from the tail (default 50)

name: claws_poll
description: Stream shell-integration command-completion events across all terminals. Pass since=cursor to get only new events. Note: unreliable in wrapped terminals — use claws_read_log instead.

since.description: Sequence cursor — return only events after this (default 0)

name: claws_close
description: Close and dispose a terminal. Always close terminals you created when the work is done.

name: claws_worker
description: Run a complete worker lifecycle in one blocking call: creates a wrapped terminal, optionally launches Claude Code with --dangerously-skip-permissions, sends the mission prompt, polls the capture buffer for a completion marker (default MISSION_COMPLETE) or error markers, harvests the final output, and auto-closes. Returns a structured result. Set detach=true for the legacy fire-and-forget behavior.

mission.description: Mission prompt sent to Claude Code. Include your completion marker (default MISSION_COMPLETE) so the blocker knows when to stop.

command.description: Alternative to mission: raw shell command sent to a wrapped terminal. Implies launch_claude=false.

launch_claude.description: Launch claude --dangerously-skip-permissions before sending mission (default: true if mission present, false if command present)

detach.description: Return immediately after spawning (legacy behavior, default false).

timeout_ms.description: Max wait for completion in ms (default 1800000 = 30 min).

boot_wait_ms.description: Max wait for Claude Code boot before sending mission (default 8000).

boot_marker.description: Substring that indicates Claude booted (default "Claude Code").

poll_interval_ms.description: How often to check the capture buffer (default 1500).

harvest_lines.description: Tail N lines of output to return on completion (default 200).

close_on_complete.description: Auto-close the terminal after completion (default true).

name: claws_hello
description: Register this Claude session with the Claws server as an orchestrator or worker peer. Must be called before subscribe, publish, or task commands. Returns a peerId for this session.

role.description: Peer role. Orchestrator may assign/cancel/broadcast; worker may publish status and claim tasks; observer is read-only.

terminalId.description: Optional: associate this peer with a specific terminal id so the server can correlate logs and do inject fan-out.

capabilities.description: Optional list of capability strings the peer advertises (server intersects with its own).

name: claws_subscribe
description: Subscribe to a topic pattern on the Claws message bus. Patterns support wildcards: '*' matches one segment, '**' matches many. Examples: 'task.status', 'worker.*', 'task.**'. Returns a subscriptionId.

topic.description: Topic pattern to subscribe to (dot-namespaced, supports * and ** wildcards).

name: claws_publish
description: Publish a payload to a topic on the Claws message bus. All peers subscribed to a matching pattern will receive a push frame.

echo.description: If true, sender also receives the message (default false).

name: claws_broadcast
description: Orchestrator-only: send a text message to all workers (or all peers). Optionally inject the text directly into each worker's terminal via bracketed paste. Useful as a kill-switch or coordination signal.

inject.description: If true, text is also sent into each peer's associated terminal via bracketed paste (default false).

name: claws_ping
description: Check that the Claws server is reachable. Returns the server's current timestamp. Useful as a heartbeat.

name: claws_peers
description: List all currently registered peers on the Claws server (claws/2 connections that have called hello). Returns role, peerName, terminalId, and lastSeen for each peer.

name: claws_lifecycle_plan
description: Log the PLAN phase — required before any claws_create call. The server-side lifecycle gate blocks terminal creation until this succeeds. Provide a 1–3 sentence summary of what you are about to do. Idempotent: safe to call twice.

plan.description: 1–3 sentence mission summary. What you are doing, why, and what success looks like.

name: claws_lifecycle_advance
description: Advance the server-side lifecycle state machine to the next phase. Legal transitions per event-protocol.md §4: PLAN→SPAWN, SPAWN→DEPLOY|RECOVER|FAILED, DEPLOY→OBSERVE|RECOVER|FAILED, OBSERVE→HARVEST|RECOVER|FAILED, RECOVER→DEPLOY|OBSERVE|FAILED, HARVEST→CLEANUP|FAILED, CLEANUP→REFLECT|FAILED. Idempotent: advancing to the current phase is a no-op.

reason.description: Optional human-readable transition reason (logged in state).

name: claws_lifecycle_snapshot
description: Read the current server-side lifecycle state without changing it. Returns null when no PLAN has been logged yet. Use this to check what phase you are in before sending lifecycle commands.

name: claws_lifecycle_reflect
description: Terminal lifecycle transition: advance to REFLECT and persist your retrospective text. Call this after CLEANUP when all workers are closed and you have assessed the session. The REFLECT phase is terminal — no further transitions are allowed.

reflect.description: Retrospective text: what succeeded, what failed, what to improve next time.

name: claws_wave_create
description: Register a new wave on the server, initialising heartbeat tracking for each expected sub-worker role. Call this as LEAD immediately after hello. waveId should be a stable human-readable slug (e.g. 'embedder-v1'). The server fires a 25s violation timer per sub-worker that resets on each heartbeat.

layers.description: Human-readable layer or goal labels this wave covers (e.g. ['L1-schemas', 'L2-handlers']).

manifest.description: Expected sub-worker roles. One violation timer is started per role.

name: claws_wave_status
description: Fetch a live snapshot of the wave: sub-worker heartbeat timestamps, completion flags, and wave-level complete/summary fields. Use this to monitor progress or diagnose silent sub-workers.

name: claws_wave_complete
description: Mark the wave as complete on the server. Clears all sub-worker violation timers. Call this after every sub-worker has published its complete event and the LEAD has committed, built, and run the full test suite. Only the peer that created the wave (LEAD) may call this.

summary.description: One-paragraph retrospective: what shipped, test result, any regressions.

commits.description: Git commit SHAs produced during this wave.

regressionClean.description: True if the full test suite passed with no regressions after the wave's changes.

name: claws_deliver_cmd
description: Deliver a typed command envelope to a specific worker peer over the pub/sub bus. The server validates the payload against the declared schema, allocates a monotonic sequence number, and pushes the command to the worker's auto-subscription. Use idempotencyKey (a UUID) to make retries safe — duplicate keys return {ok:true, duplicate:true} without re-delivering.

targetPeerId.description: peerId of the worker to receive the command.

cmdTopic.description: Full topic the server will push on (e.g. cmd.p_000002.abort).

payload.description: EnvelopeV1 payload: {v:1, id:uuid, schema:string, from_peer, from_name, ts_published, data}.

idempotencyKey.description: Client-generated UUID. Duplicate keys return {ok:true, duplicate:true} without re-delivering.

name: claws_cmd_ack
description: Acknowledge receipt and execution of a delivered command (worker-only). The server fans out a cmd.<workerPeerId>.ack event to all orchestrator subscribers. status must be one of: executed, rejected, duplicate.

seq.description: Sequence number from the deliver-cmd response.

status.description: Execution outcome reported by the worker.

correlation_id.description: Optional UUID carried through for orchestrator correlation.

name: claws_schema_list
description: Return a sorted list of all Zod schema names registered in the Claws schema registry. Use this to discover which schemas are available before calling claws_schema_get.

name: claws_schema_get
description: Return a simplified JSON representation of one registered Zod schema by name (e.g. 'worker-boot-v1', 'rpc-request-v1'). Use claws_schema_list to discover valid names.

name.description: Schema name as returned by claws_schema_list (e.g. 'worker-boot-v1').

name: claws_rpc_call
description: Issue a typed RPC call to a target peer. The server routes the call to rpc.<targetPeerId>.request and waits for the worker to publish a response to rpc.response.<callerPeerId>.<requestId>. Returns the result or a timeout error.

targetPeerId.description: peerId of the peer to call.

timeoutMs.description: Milliseconds before the call times out. Default: 5000.
